title: No subquery generation if tavily api fails

comments.body:
Sup @vikram-wald 

These are some valid points :)

2 notes on this:

a) We're moving in the direction of [persistence on the backend of the embeddings and report data](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1291#issuecomment-2746153303)

b) In the meantime, will a [custom Data Ingestion pipeline](https://docs.gptr.dev/docs/gpt-researcher/context/data-ingestion) that runs in the background perhaps meet your needs?

author: vikram-wald
body:
**Describe the bug**
Tavily api fails for long queries. To handle this we have multiple retriever option but even after using that, the subquery generation fails because it is using only first retriever. 

**To Reproduce**
Research on some query more than 400 chars. It will fail if using tavily only.
Now use multiple retriever with tavily as first retriever. It will work but with no subquery generation.

**Expected behavior**
The subquery retriever should use other retrievers also if available instead of returning empty list.

title: WebSocket is disconnected ,How to retrieve it after the disconnection

comments.body: try using this https://www.npmjs.com/package/reconnecting-websocket

@vikram-wald  Thank you
Reconnecting doesn't seem to work because the search has already ended. I was wondering how to prevent the search from ending. And enable users to retrieve this search

Sup @603810139 & @vikram-wald 

Some important points you're raising above.

We'll need to dig in deeper into how the NextJS frontend is handling websocket connections.

In short, the important elements that should be in place & need to be verified are:

a) No blocking processes on the server

b) Even as the Client and Server are doing their thing exchanging the specs of a report and the websocket logs of progress, there should also be a continous ping-pong match between them.

This parallel and continuous ping pong match is what "keeps the connection alive"

When the ping pong stops, its only a matter of time before they disconnect.

c) We should have some sort of logic to reconnect when the connection doesn't exist or is lost for some reason

My gut tells me that this best architecture to sustain that is some sort of [persistence component on the backend.](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1291#issuecomment-2746153303)

Green light for the PR if someone would like to start investigating or start building it out

body: When conducting deep research for a long time, there may be situations where WebSocket is disconnected. How to retrieve it after the disconnection

title: Needed workaround for "Error: 429 Resource has been exhausted"

comments.body:
I have solved the problem with the help from AI.

This part of the llm.py:
```python
    # create response
    for _ in range(10):  # maximum of 10 attempts
        response = await provider.get_chat_response(
            messages, stream, websocket
        )

        if cost_callback:
            llm_costs = estimate_llm_cost(str(messages), response)
            cost_callback(llm_costs)

        return response

    logging.error(f"Failed to get response from {llm_provider} API")
    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to get response from {llm_provider} API")
```


needs to get changed to something like that:
```python
# create response
    for attempt in range(10):  # maximum of 10 attempts
        if attempt > 0: # Wait time only after the first attempt
            await asyncio.sleep(delay)
            delay += 1  # Increase the delay for the next attempt
        try: # Add a try-except block to handle errors during the API call
            response = await provider.get_chat_response(
                messages, stream, websocket
            )
            if cost_callback:
                llm_costs = estimate_llm_cost(str(messages), response)
                cost_callback(llm_costs)
            return response # Return the response if successful
        except Exception as e: # Catch exceptions that may occur in provider.get_chat_response
            logging.warning(f"Attempt {attempt+1} failed with error: {e}") # Log the error
            if attempt == 9: # If the last attempt fails
                logging.error(f"Failed to get response from {llm_provider} API after 10 attempts.")
                raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to get response from {llm_provider} API after 10 attempts.") from e # Raise RuntimeError with the original exception as cause
    logging.error(f"Unreachable code reached in create_chat_completion - this should not happen") # Should normally not be reached, but for safety`
```
Now the Error: 429 (with google models) is not a big deal anymore.

Welcome @Penner10000 

Great point.

Green light for the PR.

cc: @assafelovic - some important upgrades above to the LLM chat completion helper:

The current code: At a glance, looks like it loops up to 10 times… but:

➡️ return response is inside the loop — so after the first attempt, it always returns and exits the loop, whether it worked or not.

➡️ No try/except, so if the call fails with a 429 or any other exception, it crashes out immediately — the loop never gets a chance to retry.

✅ @Penner10000's updated version does retry and includes:

 - A try/except block inside the loop

 - Logging for each failure

 - A delay before retrying

 - Only raises an error after the last attempt

So now it retries properly and handles things like rate limits (429) gracefully.

author: Penner10000
body:
Hello, 
I use a self-hosted seargnx as a search engine and for the strategic and fast llm I use gemini models. The smart model is the gpt-4o-mini. The embedding model is text-embedding-004 from google as well.

When I run a deep search, I reach the point that “Error: 429 Resource has been exhausted (e.g. check quota).” is displayed. Is there a possibility that the search is then not completely aborted, but waited for 5 seconds or similar and then continued? Because then the error should be reset.

title: FEATURE REQUEST: Allow user selection of models at query time

comments.body:
a) We're currently at the stage of considering these user stories:

Search Providers and Model Selection
 - For example: Users can choose from 10 different models, 10 different retrievers and hybrid
 - Implementation option: we can pass these parameters via the Headers object of the websocket request & store many API keys on the server so that the relevant keys are leveraged depending on the Search Provider or LLM the user passed to the Research Agent

b) Regarding User Management - [see here](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1290#issuecomment-2746161537)

author: ga-it
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently you can define three research options - FAST_LLM, SMART_LLM, and STRATEGIC_LLM
It would be exceptionally useful to be able to change these at query time

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Open-webui has probably got the functionality hierarchy right where you can define connections (i.e. endpoints), group rights against models and then the user in these groups can select the model at query time

By defining the endpoints, models are retrieved real-time via the api list functionality. New models are then available for administrators to make available to rights groups

For gpt-researcher, a further layers could mimic the rights groups into research groups - e.g. groups of fast_llm, smart_llm and strategic_llm models where the user can select which model to use to accomplish the task

I think the layering is important. We host litellm behind which we have hosted models on Ollama and paid api access to most providers.

Mostly our hosted models are sufficient, but it would be useful to include access to paid APIs on a permissioned basis.

The layering really helps and would demand a user management layer to gpt_researcher - I will put in a separate feature request for this.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
It is possible to alias models in Litellm and so alias three models for gpt_researcher there and then chnage the model in litellm, but that does not give sufficient real-time flexibility.

title: FEATURE REQUEST: Add user login and rights management

comments.body:
This is a strong point.

The stage where we're at currently is that we created these 2 npm packages:

a) [GPTR React Component](https://www.npmjs.com/package/gpt-researcher-ui)

b) [GPTR NodeJS Client](https://www.npmjs.com/package/gpt-researcher)

The benefit of that is that you can plop those components wherever you please and create abstractions on top of them to restrict access, for example.

c) I do like the idea of an example implementation of those 2 above packages - what's your take on Auth0 and Stripe, by the way - do you have your own Authentication and Payment libraries you're already thinking about?

As a potential user in a corporate environment, would something like JupyterHub be a multiuser model to look into?  There may be a variety of scenarios where some API_KEYs are shared firm-wide, while other tokens/keys are linked to a specific identity (e.g. department-level S3/Blob buckets, search engines, etc.)

> As a potential user in a corporate environment, would something like JupyterHub be a multiuser model to look into?  There may be a variety of scenarios where some API_KEYs are shared firm-wide, while other tokens/keys are linked to a specific identity (e.g. department-level S3/Blob buckets, search engines, etc.)

Welcome @dcieslak19973 

Need more info. 1 idea is to add back the "Models and Search Engines" tab into the Settings Modal.

![Screenshot_20250324-073623_YouTube.jpg](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6e65fffe-d1ac-420f-9409-9664f12df524)

But, the API keys wont be saved or available on the frontend - instead the Admin can save as many keys as he wants on the server as .env variables & then we can create some sort of interface where an admin can assign access to specific Search Engines & Models on the User or Team Level.

@dcieslak19973 & @ga-it please [feel free set a time to discuss](https://calendly.com/elisha-gptr/intro) - looking forward to it

I think OpenWebUI gets the hierarchy right.

1. User access:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e47cb9f1-6cfe-4918-8fa7-7b34e188b32f)

2. Ldap integration:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/18a56e8b-41f6-4d54-9850-4bfc32262844)

3. Group definition:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d6550b34-4af3-4d2c-a168-e778b3150dab)

4. System-wide connections:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ac76555-2ffe-47bd-a7d0-503f8191d792)

5. User connections:
 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df6dc394-e94d-4ce7-a2a4-3ea5659ae0ad)

6. Systemwide models:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4302dfc3-b090-4280-9574-ca16694f1e4d)

7. Model group rights:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2e912b7c-ee32-436a-b481-c42bb9ed3ad1)

This allows complete flexibility and control. Admins can set system wide rights and model connections. Users can be enabled to input their own model connections.

Open-WebUI allows endpoint definition (connections) and then lists all models on the endpoint where user and group access can then be defined per model.

Very interesting @ga-it 

cc @assafelovic wrapping GPTR within the Open WebUI Suite of tools seems like an interesting direction to implement user rights management and custom selection of Models and Retrievers at run time

looking forward of this implementation. It's interesting that OWUI team also looking at integrate with GPTR to enhance it's capability of Deep Research. 

https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/discussions/9321

author: ga-it
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
User management is required for a multi-user environment and if rights management for models is desired as per this request - https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1289

This should allow user addition, login access (preferably via LDAP connection), rights groups, etc

**Describe the solution you'd like**
As described in issue 1289, Open-webui's login, user and rights management is a useful starting point

As mentioned in issue 1289, user management and rights become crucial in a multi-user environment with access to expensive models

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
For straight login access, this can be actioned via reverse proxy such as Nginx, etc.

title: FEATURE REQUEST: Keep history / embedding to allow query and report rewriting

comments.body:
a) True - High Level, the documents are embedded into a [Langchain Vector Store](https://python.langchain.com/v0.2/docs/integrations/vectorstores/) on every run

We'll probably want to add support for a Vector Store like Pinecone or Mongo to preload the data into the Vector Store and run a report with that Vector Store as Context

More on that here: [Data Ingestion](https://docs.gptr.dev/docs/gpt-researcher/context/data-ingestion)

b) [Regarding chatting with previously saved reports](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1255#issuecomment-2714701803)

c) I like the idea of maintaining as much context for as long as possible within reason - I'd even go one step further to say that I would love for the project to have an advanced memory module.

For example, Langgraph put out this [memory agent](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-academy/blob/main/module-5/studio/memory_agent.py) which is able to classify any chat inputs as:

 - About the User
 - Tracking To Dos of the user
 - How the user likes things done

That could be huge in terms of personalization for the app where the memory module keeps track of: what you do for a living, what's your areas of interest, why you're interested in it, how far along you are in your education of a specific topic...

d) For A-C above: Green light for the PR if someone would like to start investigating or start building it out

author: ga-it
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When I go to the history, these reports are not available for further queries. When a report is immediately finished being written GPTR does allow query for a period (although this seems to be for a limited time?

Also, after a report has been produced, it seems like that is the perfect time to improve focus and style and ask for further research or for rewriting.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Could embeddings per report be kept until a delete request or alternatively for a period defined as a parameter?

Then, either when accessed as a historical report or after the first iteration of the report, allow for further query or further research or for rewriting.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I'm not sure there is a good alternative other than feeding a finished report in to a hybrid search, but the loss of the original search and embeddings limits the usefulness of this.

title: FEATURE REQUEST: Schedule and queue research requests

comments.body:
Welcome @ga-it 

Some interesting ideas 🤔
Thanks for a great app @ElishaKay !!

author: ga-it
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Requests take an indeterminate time. They can be very long particularly in a self hosted environment. 

Mutiple simultaneous requests can overwhelm a self hosted server particularly in a multi user environment. Simultaneous requests on API can result in hitting token limits. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Scheduling should allow requests to be diarised to run at a time. This could allow multiple requests to run overnight for example. 

Queuing should allow queuing as per rules. E.g. 1, 2, etc simultaneously with and or conditions that include which models they request is for - e.g. Allowing two simultaneous requests on 4o but only 1 on o1. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Throttling can be controlled on the backend using a proxy but this seems like an inferior solution

title: BUG: Pydantic error: Input should be a valid string

comments.body:
> but sometimes it looks like

So, the issue here is likely that [thinking models](https://ollama.com/search?c=thinking) are using tags like `<think>` in the response, so response instead of `["query 1", "query 2", "query 3"]` looks like
```
<think>
Now, how do I approach this task, let me think.
...
</think>

["query 1", "query 2", "query 3"]
```

Rather than fixing the symptom, it is probably best to address it at the point of origin. Looking briefly at the code it looks a lot of things internally strongly depend on formatting being accurate.

I wonder if something minimal can do the trick. E.g. in `GenericLLMProvider.get_chat_response`
```
# Remove <think>-like tag for reasoning models
res = re.sub(r"^<(\w+)>.*?</?\1>", "", res, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()
```

Hope someone can make a PR out of this.



@assafelovic, fyi
Have you tried modifying the prompt itself to be more deterministic?

I don't think this will work (or maybe I haven't understood your suggestion).

The prompt that is generating the result is [generated](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/516938669afe7d7a8dc1f2bddd2ac5a8cca59098/gpt_researcher/actions/query_processing.py#L52-L58) in [generate_search_queries_prompt](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/516938669afe7d7a8dc1f2bddd2ac5a8cca59098/gpt_researcher/prompts.py#L38-L81).

When this prompt is later [processed](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/516938669afe7d7a8dc1f2bddd2ac5a8cca59098/gpt_researcher/actions/query_processing.py#L61-L70) by a thinking model, it will have `<think>` tag in the response no matter the prompt. [json_repair](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/516938669afe7d7a8dc1f2bddd2ac5a8cca59098/gpt_researcher/actions/query_processing.py#L99) is of course not liking it.

If you have ollama older than few days, you can just run `ollama run qwen3:0.6b` and chat with it to see the issue in action. E.g. try `What is 2 + 2? You must respond with a number. The response should contain ONLY the number.`

If you recently updated ollama (or the model?) the issue might not be apparent in ollama UI as ollama recently updated interacting with thinking models ([blog](https://ollama.com/blog/thinking)). In this case try the following:
```
from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama

llm = ChatOllama(
    model = "qwen3:0.6b",
    base_url=os.environ["OLLAMA_BASE_URL"],
)

messages = [
    ("human", "What is 2 + 2? You must respond with a number. The response should contain ONLY the number."),
]

print(llm.invoke(messages).content)
```

To save you time, it looks like this:
```
<think>
Okay, the user is asking what 2 plus 2 is, and they want the answer in a number. Let me think. Well, 2 plus 2 is straightforward. If I add the two numbers together, it should be 4. I just need to make sure I'm not missing anything here. No, there's no other number involved, so the answer must be 4. I don't think there's any trick or hidden part here. Just basic addition. Yeah, so the correct number is 4.
</think>

4
```

For anyone who is coming here experiencing the same issue: until this is fixed in the library, I am simply working around it with a wrapper:
```python
import re

def fix_gptr_for_thinking_models():
    LLMProviderClass = gpt_researcher.llm_provider.generic.base.GenericLLMProvider
    original_get_chat_response = LLMProviderClass.get_chat_response

    async def get_chat_response(self, *args, **kwargs):
        res = await original_get_chat_response(self, *args, **kwargs)

        # Remove <think>-like tag for thinking models
        return re.sub(r"^<(\w+)>.*?</?\1>", "", res, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()

    if not getattr(LLMProviderClass, '_patched_get_chat_response', False):
        LLMProviderClass._patched_get_chat_response = True
        LLMProviderClass.get_chat_response = get_chat_response
```

author: ga-it
body:
**Describe the bug**

Pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 2 validation errors for EmbedRequest
input.str
  Input should be a valid string

That means sub_query is sometimes [''] (an empty string), and sometimes a nested list — which is causes the Pydantic EmbedRequest to choke.

**To Reproduce**
In gpt_researcher/skills/researcher.py

sub_query is expected to be a flat list of strings, but sometimes it looks like:

['Namibia bank ...', 'FICC products ...']             # ✅ correct
[['Namibia bank ...', 'FICC products ...']]           # ❌ nested list
['']                                                  # ⚠️ empty query string

The generated sub-queries (plan_research_outline) are coming back in this format:

['', ['subquery1', 'subquery2', 'subquery3']]

That leading empty string and the nested list are causing two critical issues:

SearxNG 400 Error
This happens when the empty string ('') is passed to a search URL like ...?q=&format=json, which is invalid.

Pydantic EmbedRequest Error
The nested list of strings causes embed_query(query) to receive [["str1", "str2"]] instead of a flat ["str1", "str2"], which violates its schema expecting a list of strings or a single string.

**Fix**
Replace:

content = await self.researcher.context_manager.get_similar_content_by_query(sub_query, scraped_data)

With:

# Final cast to ensure query is a plain string before embedding
if isinstance(sub_query, list):
    sub_query = " ".join([str(x) for x in sub_query if isinstance(x, str)])
elif not isinstance(sub_query, str):
    sub_query = str(sub_query)

content = await self.researcher.context_manager.get_similar_content_by_query(sub_query, scraped_data)

title: Follow-up questions broken

comments.body:
Sup @rba100 

Agreed - this is one of my top priorities this sprint.

My plan for next week is to do one more blog post: "The Road to Production" - which includes a high-level overview of taking GPTR to Production - the GPTR Docker package, npm packages, frontend embed script...

Once I close that up, I'll go deep into polishing up the NextJS app, starting with this ticket.

cc @assafelovic 

If anyone from the community wants to take a shot at this ticket in the mean time, please feel free

Let's do this 👏

Any updates about this issue?  This feature would be very valuable!  Without ability to ask followup questions the research process has to start over, which is slow and expensive especially when using report source "My Documents" or "Hybrid" because it results in local documents reindexed/embedded.  

As an additional idea, a user can continue iterating on the same research report, adding more topics, sections, or providing some clarifications in the follow-up prompts.  

I saw some code that was already started for this feature in websocket_manager.py:

```
report = await run_agent(task, report_type, report_source, source_urls, document_urls, tone, websocket, headers=headers, query_domains=query_domains, config_path=config_path)
# Create new Chat Agent whenever a new report is written
self.chat_agent = ChatAgentWithMemory(report, config_path, headers)
return report

```

author: rba100
body:
**Describe the bug**
The 'ask questions about this report' feature at the bottom of the nextjs frontend is broken.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behaviour:
1. Create a report (quick 2 min one)
2. Ask a follow-up question. The app will freeze and never perform the task.
3. Reload the page and select the report from the history panel on the left.
4. Ask the follow-up question again. In this case it fails a different way: you are taken to the homepage and no task is attempted.

**Expected behaviour**
Follow-up questions are executed.

**Additional context**
Service is run using docker compose with no modifications beyond .env file.

title: Got "IndexError: list index out of range" when running a research
author: N4SIRODDIN3
body:
**Hi there,**
I'm encountering an error when trying to conduct my research. The process was progressing until an exception was raised. title: Connection from NextJS Frontend to GPTR Backend

comments.body:
Welcome @edwin-yan 

Can you make the GPTR API URL serve over https?

For example: `https://192.168.1.250:8000/`

Your error is saying that your containers should either be both `http` or both `https`

author: edwin-yan
body: **Describe the bug** I am not sure if this is an expected behavior. I noticed that the NextJS Frontend establish connection to the GPTR Backend from the browser directly based on the "NEXT_PUBLIC_GPTR_API_URL" variable. Hence, while it works perfectly within the local network, if I want to expose the frontend (port 3000) to internet, I will have to expose the backend (port 8000) as well. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Use Docker Composer to spin up 2 containers, one for gpt-researcher and one for gptr-nextjs 2. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_GPTR_API_URL to the IP address of the host with the port, in my case, "http://192.168.1.250:8000" 3. Expose the NextJS to the internet via reverse proxy such as NGINX 4. Use NextJS Front end, and you will see the error in the console. **Expected behavior** I would expect the front end will talk to the backend via the server, not the browser. For example, my local LLM is hosted on another machine. Then, I use its IP address as the "OPENAI_API_BASE" for apt-researcher. Even if I expose port 8000 to the internet, but not my local LLM's port, the Vanilla front end will still work. **Screenshots** **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS: MacOS - Browser: Safari - Version: Latest

title: [FEATURE REQUEST] Multiple OpenAI base URLS for local hosting

comments.body: I’m facing the same issue you described. I’m using vLLM to expose all my models and embeddings, but each one has a different URL, and currently, there’s no way to configure multiple OpenAI base URLs.
Could you switch over to using LiteLLM's proxy which does support embeddings and could be configured to hit multiple OPENAI_BASE_URLs?

author: bitbyteboom
body:
Currently, it's impossible to use Openrouter as openai API along with a local custom openai api endpoint for embedding.  Openrouter doesn't do embedding models, and free google embedding model is constantly hitting rate limits. 
 Similarly, it's impossible to use a combination of different local machines or openrouter + local for the fast/smart/strategic models.  I'd happily host locally for some of the models, especially embedding, but currently it's not possible as it would need a secondary openai base url.

It would be nice to have a PRIMARY_OPENAI_BASE_URL and SECONDARY_OPENAI_BASE_URL... if not completely separate ones for Fast/Smart/Strategic/Embedding,  like CUSTOM_FAST_BASE_URL, etc.

title: Can't find local pdf documents when using pip package

comments.body:
This is most likely an issue with a missing python library.  Make sure you install all dependencies in requirements.txt and check for any DEBUG level errors about missing packages.  

I was also getting "No documents found" when loading Excel files from local folder DOC_PATH, and resolved it by adding the following additional packages to requirements.txt:
networkx
pandas
openpyxl
simsimd

Please close the issue if this issue is resolved in your dev environment.

title: Deep Research - Context Trimming Failure

comments.body: Hey @salmanjamali, I had the same issue, see here: https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/issues/1279 - it also lists a partial solution that worked, however, it neutered the deep research feature way too much.

Thanks for sharing that! In my case, there isn't any config or overrides. Just trying to make the ootb "deep" variation work.

I caught up a bit - the change was introduced here: https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/pull/1195/files
Ah, that's a shame. Let me know if you figure out a solution. I'll also look at the change you linked.
follow

@assafelovic 
# BUG: Type mismatch in trim_context_to_word_limit function call

## Problem Description
The `all_context` variable contains a nested list structure with dictionaries:

```
[
    [
        {
            "url": "",
            "title": "",
            "content": "",
        },
    ],
]
```
However, `trim_context_to_word_limit()` expects a `List[str]` and calls `count_words()` which tries to call `.split()` on each item. Since the items are dictionaries, not strings, this causes an `AttributeError` when the code tries to execute `text.split()` on a dictionary object.

## Error Location
The error occurs in:
- **File**: `gpt_researcher/skills/deep_research.py`
- **Line**: 21 in `count_words` function
- **Code**: `return len(text.split())`
- https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/58bbd6ebd7f9c2b59ebaca840aed293b0d0af90f/gpt_researcher/skills/deep_research.py#L330

## Root Cause
Type mismatch between expected input (`List[str]`) and actual input (nested list of dictionaries).

## Possible Solutions

### Solution 1: Extract content strings before calling the function
```python
# Trim context to stay within word limits
        logger.info(f"Total context items before trimming: {len(all_context)}")
        # Flatten all_context and extract 'content' fields from each dictionary
        content_strings = [
            item.get("content", "")
            for sublist in all_context
            for item in (sublist if isinstance(sublist, list) else [sublist])
            if isinstance(item, dict) and "content" in item
        ]
        trimmed_context = trim_context_to_word_limit(content_strings)
        logger.info(
            f"Trimmed context from {len(all_context)} items to {len(trimmed_context)} items to stay within word limit"
        )
```

### Solution 2: Modify trim_context_to_word_limit to handle nested dictionary structure
Update the function to properly extract and process the content from the dictionary structure.

## Impact
This bug prevents the context trimming functionality from working, which could lead to context overflow issues in the research process.

title: My Documents Mode pull information from the internet
author: migumnakkittikul
body:
**Describe the bug**
When using GPTResearcher in "Documents Mode", the generated summaries include information and sources that appear to be pulled from the internet, rather than solely from the uploaded documents. This contradicts the expected behavior of "Documents Mode", which should restrict content generation to the provided documents only.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Go to GPTResearcher and enable "Documents Mode"

2. Upload a set of documents into the document folder

3. Run the summarization task

4. Review the output summary

5. Observe that the summary includes external sources and references not present in the uploaded files

**Expected behavior**
The summary should be generated strictly based on the content of the uploaded documents. No information from the internet or external sources should appear in the output when "Documents Mode" is enabled.

**Screenshots**
This is the screenshot containing the part where the information from the internet (not included in all 30 documents uploaded) is contained in the summary:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/975c103c-ef2b-435b-a8a4-e8aeed2ce55b)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5cb64804-793e-4084-85b6-90f964b22376)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2451434e-3ea8-4e87-a3cc-30104dbbe95c)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a457c5db-dee0-4097-a77b-dd3e7fe0278b)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Windows
 - Browser Chrome
 - Version 135

**List of the documents uploaded:**

TSQ: An Optimized Framework for Efficiently Answering Time Series Queries
Authors: Feifan Pu, Jianqiu Xu
Year: 2025

Query Optimization in Distributed Database Based on Improved Artificial Bee Colony Algorithm
Authors: Yan Du, Zhi Cai, Zhiming Ding
Year: 2024

Learned Offline Query Planning via Bayesian Optimization
Authors: Jeffrey Tao, Natalie Maus, Haydn Jones, Yimeng Zeng, Jacob R. Gardner, Ryan Marcus
Year: 2025

Can Large Language Models Be Query Optimizer for Relational Databases?
Authors: Jie Tan, Kangfei Zhao, Rui Li, Jeffrey Xu Yu, Chengzhi Piao, Hong Cheng, Helen Meng, Deli Zhao, Yu Rong
Year: 2025

Neo: A Learned Query Optimizer
Authors: Ryan Marcus, Parimarjan Negi, Hongzi Mao, Chi Zhang, Mohammad Alizadeh, Tim Kraska, Olga Papaemmanouil, Nesime Tatbul

Query Optimization Through the Looking Glass, and What We Found Running the Join Order Benchmark

Year: ~2018

Apache Calcite: A Foundational Framework for Optimized Query Processing Over Heterogeneous Data Sources

title: How can i use local ollama instead of openAI api?

comments.body:
Add this to your .env file and use what ever model you have downloaded:

```
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434
FAST_LLM="ollama:gemma3:27b-it-qat"
SMART_LLM="ollama:gemma3:27b-it-qat"
STRATEGIC_LLM="ollama:gemma3:27b-it-qat"

EMBEDDING="ollama:nomic-embed-text"
```

Hi @hhajj! 

If you're looking for a workflow tool that supports **local Ollama** with a great visual interface, you should check out **n8n** (https://n8n.io):

- ✅ Native Ollama integration (no API key needed for local models)
- ✅ Visual workflow builder for AI automations  
- ✅ Connect Ollama to 400+ other services
- ✅ Self-host locally or use cloud

n8n makes it super easy to chain local LLMs with other automation tasks. Would love to help you get started!

author: hhajj

title: Better leveral Large Context Length LLMs
author: dcieslak19973
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
With larger context window models (1M token) becoming more prevalent like Gemini and GPT-41, would it make sense to review places in code where content is truncated to a hard-coded length and allow the user to configure truncation a bit more?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Allowing larger context length models to get more content from documents to take advantage of their larger context windows

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**
Admittedly, have not done a recent code review, though I do recall seeing hard-coded content-length truncation in the code base when I last looked about a month ago

title: API Reference Docs

comments.body:
Hey @maxwelljmckee thanks for reaching out!
You can check the docs here: https://docs.gptr.dev

Hey @assafelovic, thanks so much for your reply and for putting together these docs! I've read through them quite a bit (though I'm sure there's still plenty for me to learn).

The current docs are really helpful as a "How to" guide, rather than a technical reference material. But I'm wondering if there's any roadmap to provide explicit api reference docs, such as the exact input/output contracts for the library's exports. 

For example, it would be awesome to have an exhaustive list of all the configuration kwargs for GPTResearcher, along with the exact data types they expect (like knowing that `report_type` must be one of a finite set of values).

A lot of this info is in the current documentation, but it's spread out, and it can be tricky to remember where I read something. A consolidated API reference would be incredibly helpful for anyone using the tool.

Thanks and again, much respect for the work you're doing 🏆

author: maxwelljmckee
body:
First of all, this is an incredible tool and I am absolutely amazed by its capabilities. Before discovering your work through Tavily's documentation, I was considering scratch-building a deep-research agent of my own, and I'm so grateful to have your library to build from.

My question is this: is there any chance of getting more comprehensive api reference docs on your website? I'm struggling to piece together all the available configuration options from the more "how it works" style docs.
